rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE BLANDINA D. PADLAN REVOCABLE TRUST OF 5/16/00 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Under Code Civ Proc. 1008 For Order Denying Motion To Sanction Entered Aug. 2, 2019
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. The Motion for Reconsideration Under CCP § 1008 for Order Denying Motion to sanction Entered August 2, 2019 is denied. Procedurally, this motion is defective because it does not comply with CCP § 1008: "The party making the application shall state by affidavit what application was made before, when and to what judge, what order or decisions were made, and what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP § 1008(a)). Ms. Graves has submitted a declaration with this motion, but it does not comply with any part of this statutory requirement. Substantively, the only relevant argument offered in support of the motion under CCP § 1008 is that the statutory safe harbor period was observed before the Motion for Sanctions was filed. As discussed in the August 2, 2019 Order, the motion for sanctions under § 127.8 is improperly brought after the Court has entered a dispositive ruling on the underlying petition: "The law is well settled that the [CCP § 127.8] safe harbor provisions preclude a party from seeking monetary sanctions for an offending pleading following a dispositive ruling by the trial court on the pleading." (Malovec v. Hamrell (1999) 70 Cal. App. 4th 434, 441, citing Goodstone v. Southwest Airlines Co. (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 406; Cromwell v. Cummings (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th Supp. 10; Barber v. Miller (9th Cir.1998) 146 F.3d 707; Ridder v. City of Springfield (1997) 109 F.3d 288.) A dispositive order was entered on the underlying petition on March 14, 2019. Exhibit A of this motion shows service of the motion for sanctions in May 2019. The motion for sanctions under CCP § 127.8 was improperly brought after the Court entered a dispositive ruling on the underlying petition, as the Court determined in the August 2, 2019 Order. Further, the safe harbor issue was listed in the Court's tentative ruling on the motion for sanctions, and the moving party could have offered this argument before the August 2, 2019 Order was entered. This information is not new law, facts, or circumstances, as required by CCP § 1008. Ms. Graves offers no argument as to why this information was not offered at the hearing, although her motion specifically discusses this requirement (at page 14, lines 1-13). The other arguments presented in this Motion regarding reconsideration of the Court's August 2, 2019 Order denying Ms. Graves' Motion for Sanctions Under CCP § 127.8 Against Attorney Harald Westendorf, and Motion for Costs and Fees Against Alexis Padlan are also not new law, facts, or circumstances as required by CCP § 1008, and so are irrelevant for the purposes of this motion. The motion otherwise attempts to assail completely different orders, as well as the underlying petition which has already been submitted on the papers and determined. This is frivolous and improper as part of a motion for reconsideration of the August 2, 2019 Order. Petitioner Alexis Padlan shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 17, 2019.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHUNG 1994 REVOCABLE INTERVIVOS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Chung 1994 Revocable Intervivos Trust, Dated February 15, 1994
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/30/19. (No earlier date available.) The petition does not set forth any reason for modifying the trust and does not address why only one settlor/trustee is petitioning. There is reference to a prior amendment dated 8/13/16, which the petition also does not address. No copy of that amendment was attached as an exhibit to the petition. Prob. C. Section 15404(a) does not apply in this instance as a settlor is deceased. The reference to "articles one throught nineteen" in paragrpah E of the proposed Second Amendment does not appear to have any corresponding provisions in the 1994 trust. No copy of the proof of service was provided to the Court (Examiner).
